divergence: The sun was privatized and daylight became a premium tier
story_angle: deadpan report of a catastrophe that everyone involved considers a great success
headline: Lord Methuselah47 Proves Darkness Profitable as Eclipse Shields Sell Like Hotcakes
deck: Serfs Offer Up Their Dusk for a Whiff of Dawn as Lords Rake in the Gold.
article:
Across the brass-riveted terraces of Jovion, Lord Methuselah47's petition has locked in a new era of paid total blackout. Forty-seven fresh eclipse licenses now trade at triple prior value. The Solar Council’s nod has sent serfs scrambling; some now tender 12,000 symmetry tokens for a single hour of sunlight.

Isaac9, Grand Vizier of Radiance, called the blackout orderly while auctioning twilight to the highest bidder at the Royal Marketplace. Serfdom has become an auction sport, lowest classes bidding against one another for scraps of dawn. 'Plenty of daylight remains,' Isaac9 noted, 'for those who can afford it.'

Shield patents have sparked fresh bureaucratic scrambles as lords chase extra defense licenses and push darkness-relief taxes onto commoners. Each sunless day fattens aristocratic coffers; commoners mutter beneath the pall. With dawn now a traded good, talk turns to whether the next petition will slap tariffs on starlight.
author: Gideon7, Scribe of the Solar Council

weather.condition: Eclipse coupons now available for purchase

oped.title: Eclipse Shields: A Beacon of Enterprise the Serfs Ungratefully Eclipse
oped.author: Viscount Horace Blunderbuss, Royal Eclipse Auditor and Proprietor of the Moonlit Hat Emporium
oped.body: Lord Methuselah47’s eclipse shields mark the summit of feudal enterprise, turning simple absence of light into coin that any sensible noble should envy. Instead of grumbling when serfs trade their dusk, we ought to praise the market for teaching them dawn’s true price—payable in advance. Their grumbling arises less from darkness than from hat negligence; broader brims would have spared them premium rays years ago. I floated similar veils last harvest and received no patents, confirming that timing, not fairness, decides victory. The crown should now impose dusk quotas on every hovel so lesser lords might finally recover losses from obsolete torch futures. Progress always exacts payment, preferably from those already practiced in shadow.

classifieds.text: FOR SALE: Three jars of fresh dusk, pressed from my own fields by Serf Eldred. Will swap for one dawnbeam or best offer in eclipse wax, as my turnips now grow blind.
HELP WANTED: Lord Methuselah47 seeks two strong backs to polish eclipse shields by torchlight alone. Pay is one sniff of dawn weekly; bring thine own chains.
PERSONALS: Desperate widow of the east hamlet offers her remaining twilight hours to any knight who can spare a half-hour of true daylight for her ailing babe. No lords need reply.
PUBLIC NOTICE: I, Bailiff Thorne, do publicly confess to pocketing three serfs' dusk payments and hereby return one vial in hopes the new sunlords overlook mine greed come audit.

panels.caption: Lord Methuselah47 hawks from his stall: 'Eclipse shields! Block the free sun, pay for proper darkness!'
Serf offers a clay token: 'My dusk hours for one whiff of dawn, m'lord.'
Methuselah47: 'Accepted. Your nights are now leased back at triple rate.'

joke.setup: The bailiff found a peasant selling eclipse shields by torchlight
joke.punchline: and fined him for unlicensed darkness after the lord had already claimed the patent on night.
joke.text: The bailiff found a peasant selling eclipse shields by torchlight and fined him for unlicensed darkness after the lord had already claimed the patent on night.

sponsor_ads.headline: Methuselah47 Eclipse Shields: Darkness by Royal Patent
sponsor_ads.body: Shield thy hovel from the privatized sun's rays and join the profitable gloom; each shield sold yields a farthing to the Lord of Perpetual Night.

sponsor_ads.headline: Daylight Tithe Collectors Seek Moonless Tenants
sponsor_ads.body: Rent thy allotted hours of sun at guild rates or default to endless shade; Lord Methuselah47's ledgers prove night pays better than noon.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Arrears settled in eclipse scrip only.

sponsor_ads.headline: Nocturnal Crop Seeds: Grow Without Paying the Sun
sponsor_ads.body: Sow these kernels beneath thy purchased darkness and reap yields that mock the daylight monopoly; profits rise as the orb is dimmed.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Warranty void if any ray touches soil.

sponsor_ads.headline: Public Notice: Sunlight Debtors to Face Total Eclipse
sponsor_ads.body: Those behind on premium rays must don the new shields or forfeit all light; Lord Methuselah47's coffers swell with each unpaid dawn.

stories.headline: Barley Tithes Fall to Zero as Eclipse Licenses Blanket the Terraces
stories.deck: Lords Methuselah47 and Ezekiel19 record record levies on shadow acreage.
stories.article:
Copper-riveted terraces that once glowed with Jovion barley now sit under purchased gloom. Serfs who surrendered dusk quotas watch stalks wither and granaries empty after one day of licensed night.

Bailiffs tally lost bushels as taxable shade. Lords demand payment in remaining dawn hours before any replanting under permitted light.

stories.headline: Gang of Unlicensed Dawn Thieves Apprehended at the Brass Rivet Markets
stories.deck: Three serfs caught siphoning premium rays into smuggled vials face the eclipse stocks.
stories.article:
Night watchmen seized a copper flask holding two stolen minutes of first light near the Halcyon Gate. The vial carried Lord Methuselah47’s seal and was bound for black-market sale to field hands.

Magistrates fitted the culprits with permanent dusk collars until their next tithe clears. Further raids target any household still showing unpermitted glow.

stories.headline: Temple of the Halcyon Sun Raises Indulgence Fees for Dawn Prayers
stories.deck: Priest Isaiah07 cites rising eclipse tithes as reason for doubled offerings.
stories.article:
Worshippers at the central copper nave now surrender three minutes of personal daylight for one verse of morning benediction. The temple ledger shows shadow-right collections have tripled since Lord Methuselah47’s first shield patent.

Those short of the rate are steered to side chapels for perpetual-dusk supplicants. Evening services have been trimmed to conserve licensed illumination.

real_world_echo: mirrors Zelensky pressing Nato for air defence after Russian strikes, recast as a feudal petition to the Solar Council for light shields after 'premium strikes' of withheld dawn
